back_translation: And the LORD sent Nathan to David, and he came to him and said to him, 'There were two men in a certain city, one rich and one poor.
To the rich man there were sheep and cattle in great abundance,

but to the poor man there was nothing, except one small ewe lamb which he had bought; and he kept it alive, and it grew up with him and with his sons together. From his morsel it would eat, and from his cup it would drink, and in his bosom it would lie; and it was to him like a daughter.

And a traveler came to the rich man, and he spared to take from his own flock and from his own herd to prepare for the wayfarer who had come to him; and he took the ewe lamb of the poor man and prepared it for the man who had come to him.'
And David's anger burned greatly against the man, and he said to Nathan, 'As the LORD lives — a son of death is the man who has done this!
And the ewe lamb he shall pay fourfold, because he did this thing and because he had no pity.'
And Nathan said to David, 'You are the man. Thus says the LORD God of Israel: I anointed you king over Israel, and I delivered you from the hand of Saul.

And I gave to you the house of your master and the wives of your master into your bosom, and I gave to you the house of Israel and Judah; and if it were little, I would have added to you like these and like these.

Why have you despised the word of the LORD by doing what is evil in His sight? Uriah the Hittite you struck with the sword, and his wife you took to be your wife; and him you killed with the sword of the sons of Ammon.
And now, the sword shall not depart from your house forever, because you have despised Me and have taken the wife of Uriah the Hittite to be your wife.'

Thus says the LORD: 'Behold, I raise up against you evil from your own house, and I will take your wives before your eyes and give them to your neighbor, and he shall lie with your wives in the sight of this sun.
For you did it in secret, but I will do this thing before all Israel and before the sun.'
And David said to Nathan, 'I have sinned against the LORD.' And Nathan said to David, 'The LORD also has caused your sin to pass; you shall not die.
Nevertheless, because you have utterly scorned the enemies of the LORD by this thing, also the son born to you shall surely die.'
And Nathan went to his house, and the LORD struck the child whom Uriah's wife had borne to David, and he became ill.
And David pleaded with God for the child, and David fasted a fast, and went and spent the night and lay on the ground.
And the elders of his house arose over him to raise him from the ground, but he was unwilling and did not eat bread with them.

And it happened on the seventh day, that the child died. And the servants of David feared to tell him that the child was dead, for they said, 'Behold, while the child was alive we spoke to him and he did not listen to our voice. How then can we say to him, The child is dead? He might do evil.'
And David saw that his servants were whispering, and David understood that the child was dead. And David said to his servants, 'Is the child dead?' And they said, 'Dead.'

And David arose from the ground and washed and anointed himself, and changed his garments, and came to the house of the LORD and worshiped; and he came to his own house, and asked, and they set bread before him, and he ate.
And his servants said to him, 'What is this thing that you have done? For the sake of the child being alive you fasted and wept, but when the child died, you arose and ate bread.'
And he said, 'While the child was still alive I fasted and wept, for I said, Who knows? The LORD may be gracious to me and the child shall live.
But now he is dead. Why should I fast? Can I bring him back again? I am going to him, but he will not return to me.'
And David comforted Bathsheba his wife, and he came to her and lay with her; and she bore a son, and called his name Solomon; and the LORD loved him.
And He sent by the hand of Nathan the prophet, and called his name Jedidiah, for the LORD's sake.
And Joab fought against Rabbah of the sons of Ammon, and captured the royal city.
And Joab sent messengers to David and said, 'I have fought against Rabbah; also I have captured the city of waters.
And now, gather the rest of the people, and encamp against the city and capture it, lest I capture the city and my name be called over it.'
And David gathered all the people and went to Rabbah, and fought against it and captured it.

And he took the crown of their king from off his head — and its weight was a talent of gold, with precious stone — and it was on David's head; and the spoil of the city he brought out in great abundance.

And the people who were in it he brought out, and set with the saw and with iron picks and with iron axes, and made them pass through the brick-kiln. And thus he did to all the cities of the sons of Ammon. And David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.
